channel.title: Chart Breakers & Star Stories
channel.itunes:author: Family TVR
channel.itunes:summary: Did your favorite artist just break a record or a heart? The charts are screaming, and we have the inside story you won't hear anywhere else.
channel.description: The latest music trends, chart updates, and star stories from Billboard and beyond. Stay updated with the Pop Culture.

item.title: Andrew Lloyd Webber Says He’s a Recovering Alcoholic
item.description:
[Intro music plays]
Host: Welcome to Trending on Billboard. Today, we're talking about a legendary composer who's opening up about his personal struggles. Andrew Lloyd Webber, the 78-year-old EGOT winner, has been speaking candidly about his addiction to alcohol.
[Soft music continues in the background]
Host: In a recent interview with The Times, Lloyd Webber shared that he started his journey to sobriety over 16 months ago. He admitted that checking into a rehab clinic didn't work for him, but attending AA meetings has been a game-changer. He said, "I am a recovering alcoholic... and it's the best thing that ever happened to me."
[Short pause]
Host: Lloyd Webber had previously talked about quitting drinking in 2015 and 2016, but he started drinking again. He shared that he was "white-knuckling" it, trying to quit on his own without any support, and eventually started drinking secretly. He realized he needed help when his family was in a desperate state, and his wife was feeling like she couldn't go on.
[Soft music continues]
Host: Lloyd Webber also talked about how he used to think of himself as a wine drinker, not an alcoholic. But when he started drinking vodka to hide it, he realized he had a problem. He's grateful that nothing serious happened, but he's aware of the "near misses."
[Short pause]
Host: One of the most inspiring parts of Lloyd Webber's story is his experience with AA meetings. He attends daily meetings and has made friends he never would have met otherwise. He said, "What I love about it is you go into a room and everybody's equal."
[Upbeat music starts to play]
Host: And the good news is that Lloyd Webber is back to doing what he loves – creating music. He's currently working on two new musicals, one based on the film The Illusionist and the other on the theft of the Mona Lisa.
[Outro music plays]
Host: That's all for today's episode of Trending on Billboard. Thanks for tuning in to Andrew Lloyd Webber's powerful story of recovery and creativity. We'll be back with more trending news soon.
